mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This negative effect on S&P 500 forward returns strengthens over a 20-day horizon as market reprices earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% in Sept 2017 reflects tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns exceeds +0.05 (positive correlation), or SPX produces negative returns in fewer than 45% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) in a quarterly expansion cycle signals robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17031.1B in Q2 2017 reflects ongoing expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02, or positive GDP growth quarters produce negative SPX returns in more than 40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crossing above 1.5% signals entry into a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and increases refinancing costs for corporations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.15% in Sept 2017, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.5%, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth acceleration combined with rising capacity utilization signals supply-side strength and pricing power. When INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL inflation remains moderate, equity valuations expand over a 5-day horizon as margin expansion is priced in.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.739 in Aug 2017, CPIAUCSL at 246.373 in Sept 2017
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (INDPRO growth × inflation gap) falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth deceleration below 0.3% month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades. This negative demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is positive and exceeds 0.03, or negative returns occur in fewer than 48% of instances when RSAFS growth falls below 0.3%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) surge above 1200K units combined with rising real GDP signals strong construction demand and multiplier effects through the economy. This dual demand signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1200K units AND GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1180K in Aug 2017, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (HOUST level × GDPC1 growth) falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth acceleration above 200K persons per month combined with declining unemployment rate signals a tight labor market that raises wage inflation expectations. This dual labor tightening signal produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as real discount rates rise and margin compression is priced in.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth > 200K AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146659K in Sept 2017, UNRATE at 4.2%
regime_hint: wage acceleration cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (PAYEMS growth × UNRATE decline) is positive and exceeds 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in fewer than 48% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.0% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This regime of negative real rates produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as equity valuations expand relative to bonds, but the mechanism reverses when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0%.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 246.373 in Sept 2017, FEDFUNDS at 1.15%
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC of the real rate proxy (CPIAUCSL growth - FEDFUNDS level) falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of triggered instances when both conditions are met.
